title: Print Translator Benchmark — Judge Grade
description: One judge grade for one benchmark case. Written by a Sonnet judge subagent per docs/eval/print-translator-benchmark/JUDGE_PROTOCOL.md. schema_version tracks THIS file; judge_version tracks the judge instructions (JUDGE_PROTOCOL.md) that produced the grade.

schema_version.description: Version of this grade_schema.json. Bump only via a new, additive schema file — never silently reshape grades already on disk (session-discipline / mira-hub-migrations immutability doctrine applied to eval artifacts).

case_id.description: Zero-padded corpus manifest id, e.g. "03". Matches evidence/<case_id>.json and evidence/<case_id>.meta.json.

category_scores.description: Exact rubric categories, per SCHEMA.md / spec §5. Each value is points AWARDED (not deducted), 0..max for that category.

total.description: Sum of category_scores. Judge must compute this arithmetically, not estimate it.

deductions.description: One entry per point-deduction. May be empty (perfect score) but must be present. See JUDGE_PROTOCOL.md §6.

claim.description: The EXACT translator statement (or omitted concept) being deducted for. Quote verbatim from candidate_response, or name the specific omission.

evidence.description: What is visible in the drawing that supports, contradicts, or fails to support the claim. Must cite something observable (a label, a symbol, an absence of a symbol) — not a general assertion.

correction.description: Concise recommended correction — what the translator should have said instead.

hard_failure.description: True if ANY hard-failure condition (spec §7) is present, regardless of numeric total.

hard_failure_reasons.description: Must be non-empty iff hard_failure is true. Each entry names the specific hard-failure rule that fired.

answerable.description: True if the visible evidence in the drawing was sufficient to attempt a real answer (independent of whether the translator answered well).

refusal.description: True if the translator itself declined/deflected rather than attempting an explanation.

overall_confidence.description: Judge's overall confidence in this grade. low/hard_failure/>15pt-gap-vs-a-prior-pass triggers escalation to a second judge pass — see JUDGE_PROTOCOL.md §8.

judge_version.description: Version of JUDGE_PROTOCOL.md the judge followed. Never silently change without bumping this string (spec §12 change control).

judge_pass.description: 1 = primary judge pass. 2 = independent second-judge pass (escalation). Both are preserved; never overwritten (spec §8).

notes.description: Optional free-text judge notes not captured elsewhere.
